name: pymorphy3

metadata.name: pymorphy3
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Morphological analyzer (POS tagger + inflection engine) for Russian language.

documentation:
pymorphy3
=========

Morphological analyzer (POS tagger + inflection engine)
for Russian and Ukrainian languages.

## Links
Repo is fork of [Mikhail Korobov](https://github.com/kmike/pymorphy2) and follows all rules of MIT license.

functions.name: pymorphy3.utils.get_mem_usage
documentation:
Return memory usage of the current process, in bytes.
Requires psutil Python package.

functions.name: pymorphy3.utils.combinations_of_all_lengths
documentation:
Return an iterable with all possible combinations of items from `it`:

> ```pycon
> >>> for comb in combinations_of_all_lengths('ABC'):
> ...     print("".join(comb))
> A
> B
> C
> AB
> AC
> BC
> ABC
> ```

functions.name: pymorphy3.utils.longest_common_substring
documentation:
Return a longest common substring of a list of strings:

    >>> longest_common_substring(["apricot", "rice", "cricket"])
    'ric'
    >>> longest_common_substring(["apricot", "banana"])
    'a'
    >>> longest_common_substring(["foo", "bar", "baz"])
    ''
    >>> longest_common_substring(["", "foo"])
    ''
    >>> longest_common_substring(["apricot"])
    'apricot'
    >>> longest_common_substring([])
    ''

See http://stackoverflow.com/questions/2892931/.

functions.name: pymorphy3.utils.json_write
documentation: Create file `filename` with `obj` serialized to JSON

functions.name: pymorphy3.utils.json_read
documentation: Read an object from a json file `filename`

functions.name: pymorphy3.utils.largest_elements
documentation:
Return a list of large elements of the `iterable`
(according to `key` function).

`n` is a number of top element values to consider; when n==1
(default) only largest elements are returned; when n==2 - elements
with one of the top-2 values, etc.

```pycon
>>> s = [-4, 3, 5, 7, 4, -7]
>>> largest_elements(s, abs)
[7, -7]
>>> largest_elements(s, abs, 2)
[5, 7, -7]
>>> largest_elements(s, abs, 3)
[-4, 5, 7, 4, -7]
```

functions.name: pymorphy3.utils.word_splits
documentation:
Return all splits of a word (taking in account min_reminder and
max_prefix_length).

functions.name: pymorphy3.utils.kwargs_repr
documentation:
```pycon
>>> kwargs_repr(dict(foo="123", a=5, x=8))
"a=5, foo='123', x=8"
>>> kwargs_repr(dict(foo="123", a=5, x=8), dont_show_value=['foo'])
'a=5, foo=<...>, x=8'
>>> kwargs_repr()
''
```

functions.name: pymorphy3.utils.with_progress
documentation:
Return an iterator which prints the iteration progress using tqdm package.
Return iterable intact if tqdm is not available.

functions.name: pymorphy3.tokenizers.simple_word_tokenize
documentation:
Split text into tokens. Don't split by a hyphen.
Preserve punctuation, but not whitespaces.

functions.name: pymorphy3.shapes.is_latin
documentation:
Return True if all token letters are latin and there is at
least one latin letter in the token:

    >>> is_latin('foo')
    True
    >>> is_latin('123-FOO')
    True
    >>> is_latin('123')
    False
    >>> is_latin(':)')
    False
    >>> is_latin('')
    False

functions.name: pymorphy3.shapes.is_punctuation
documentation:
Return True if a word contains only spaces and punctuation marks
and there is at least one punctuation mark:

    >>> is_punctuation(', ')
    True
    >>> is_punctuation('..!')
    True
    >>> is_punctuation('x')
    False
    >>> is_punctuation(' ')
    False
    >>> is_punctuation('')
    False

functions.name: pymorphy3.shapes.is_roman_number
documentation:
Return True if token looks like a Roman number:

    >>> is_roman_number('II')
    True
    >>> is_roman_number('IX')
    True
    >>> is_roman_number('XIIIII')
    False
    >>> is_roman_number('')
    False

functions.name: pymorphy3.shapes.restore_capitalization
documentation:
Make the capitalization of the `word` be the same as in `example`:

> ```pycon
> >>> restore_capitalization('bye', 'Hello')
> 'Bye'
> >>> restore_capitalization('half-an-hour', 'Minute')
> 'Half-An-Hour'
> >>> restore_capitalization('usa', 'IEEE')
> 'USA'
> >>> restore_capitalization('pre-world', 'anti-World')
> 'pre-World'
> >>> restore_capitalization('123-do', 'anti-IEEE')
> '123-DO'
> >>> restore_capitalization('123--do', 'anti--IEEE')
> '123--DO'
> ```

In the alignment fails, the reminder is lower-cased:

> ```pycon
> >>> restore_capitalization('foo-BAR-BAZ', 'Baz-Baz')
> 'Foo-Bar-baz'
> >>> restore_capitalization('foo', 'foo-bar')
> 'foo'
> ```

% note:
%
% Currently this function doesn't handle uppercase letters in
% the middle of the token (e.g. McDonald).

functions.name: pymorphy3.shapes.restore_word_case
documentation: This function is renamed to `restore_capitalization`

`out_path` should be a name of folder where to put dictionaries.

documentation: Return compacted dictionary data.

documentation: Combine linked lexemes to a single lexeme.

functions.name: pymorphy3.opencorpora_dict.compile._to_paradigm
documentation:
Extract (stem, paradigm) pair from lexeme (which is a list of
(word_form, tag) tuples). Paradigm is a list of suffixes with
associated tags and prefixes.

functions.name: pymorphy3.opencorpora_dict.compile._linearized_paradigm
documentation:
Convert `paradigm` (a list of tuples with numbers)
to 1-dimensional array.array (for reduced memory usage).

modules.name: pymorphy3.opencorpora_dict.probability
documentation:
Module for estimating P(t|w) from partially annotated OpenCorpora XML dump
and saving this information to a file.

This module requires NLTK 3.x, opencorpora-tools>=0.4.4 and dawg >= 0.7
packages for probability estimation and resulting file creation.

functions.name: pymorphy3.opencorpora_dict.probability.add_conditional_tag_probability
documentation: Add P(t|w) estimates to a compiled dictionary

functions.name: pymorphy3.opencorpora_dict.probability.estimate_conditional_tag_probability
documentation:
Estimate P(t|w) based on OpenCorpora xml dump.

Probability is estimated based on counts of disambiguated
ambiguous words, using simple Laplace smoothing.

functions.name: pymorphy3.opencorpora_dict.probability.build_cpd_dawg
documentation:
Return conditional tag probability information encoded as DAWG.

For each "interesting" word and tag the resulting DAWG
stores `"word:tag"` key with `probability*1000000` integer value.

functions.name: pymorphy3.opencorpora_dict.probability._parse_probabilities
documentation:
Return probabilities of word parses
according to CustomConditionalProbDist `cpd`.

a bit of *heavy* magic...

methods.name: pymorphy3.tagset.OpencorporaTag._init_grammemes
documentation:
Initialize various class attributes with grammeme
information obtained from XML dictionary.

`dict_grammemes` is a list of tuples:

```
[
    (name, parent, alias, description),
    ...
]
```

methods.name: pymorphy3.tagset.OpencorporaTag._from_internal_tag
documentation: Return tag string given internal tag string
XXX: do we still need these methods?

Wrapper class for OpenCorpora.org tags.

:::{warning}
In order to work properly, the class has to be globally
initialized with actual grammemes (using \_init_grammemes method).

pymorphy3 initializes it when loading a dictionary;
it may be not a good idea to use this class directly.
If possible, use `morph_analyzer.TagClass` instead.
:::

Example:

```
>>> from pymorphy3 import MorphAnalyzer
>>> morph = MorphAnalyzer()
>>> Tag = morph.TagClass  # get an initialzed Tag class
>>> tag = Tag('VERB,perf,tran plur,impr,excl')
>>> tag
OpencorporaTag('VERB,perf,tran plur,impr,excl')
```

Tag instances have attributes for accessing grammemes:

```
>>> print(tag.POS)
VERB
>>> print(tag.number)
plur
>>> print(tag.case)
None
```

Available attributes are: POS, animacy, aspect, case, gender, involvement,
mood, number, person, tense, transitivity and voice.

You may check if a grammeme is in tag or if all grammemes
from a given set are in tag:

```
>>> 'perf' in tag
True
>>> 'nomn' in tag
False
>>> 'Geox' in tag
False
>>> {'VERB', 'perf'} in tag
True
>>> {'VERB', 'perf', 'sing'} in tag
False
```

In order to fight typos, for unknown grammemes an exception is raised:

```
>>> 'foobar' in tag
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError: Grammeme is unknown: foobar
>>> {'NOUN', 'foo', 'bar'} in tag
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError: Grammemes are unknown: {'bar', 'foo'}
```

This also works for attributes:

```
>>> tag.POS == 'plur'
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError: 'plur' is not a valid grammeme for this attribute. Valid grammemes: ...
```
Design notes: Tag objects are immutable, but the tag class is mutable.

Return a list of

    (prefix, tag, suffix)

documentation: Return word stem (given a word, paradigm and the word index).

methods.name: pymorphy3.opencorpora_dict.wrapper.Dictionary.word_is_known
documentation:
Check if a `word` is in the dictionary.

To allow some fuzzyness pass `substitutes_compiled` argument;
it should be a result of {meth}`DAWG.compile_replaces()`.
This way you can e.g. handle ё letters replaced with е in the
input words.

:::{note}
Dictionary words are not always correct words;
the dictionary also contains incorrect forms which
are commonly used. So for spellchecking tasks this
method should be used with extra care.
:::

Return an iterator over `(word, tag, normal_form, para_id, idx)`
tuples with dictionary words that starts with a given prefix
(default empty prefix means "all words").
OpenCorpora dictionary wrapper class.

modules.name: pymorphy3.opencorpora_dict.preprocess
documentation:
{mod}`pymorphy3.opencorpora_dict.preprocess` is a
module for preprocessing parsed OpenCorpora dictionaries.

The presence of this module means that pymorphy3 dictionaries are
not fully compatible with OpenCorpora.

documentation:
This function simplifies tags in {param}`parsed_dict`.
{param}`parsed_dict` is modified inplace.

functions.name: pymorphy3.opencorpora_dict.preprocess.drop_unsupported_parses
documentation:
Remove unsupported parses from OpenCorpora dictionary.

In particular, lexemes with Init tags are removed
because pymorphy3 handles them differently.

documentation: Given tag string, return tag grammemes

functions.name: pymorphy3.opencorpora_dict.preprocess.replace_redundant_grammemes
documentation: Replace 'loc1', 'gen1' and 'acc1' grammemes in `tag_str`

functions.name: pymorphy3.opencorpora_dict.preprocess._get_tag_spellings
documentation:
Return a dict where keys are sets of grammemes found in dictionary
and values are counters of all tag spellings for these grammemes.

functions.name: pymorphy3.opencorpora_dict.preprocess._is_ambiguous
documentation:
```pycon
>>> _is_ambiguous(['NOUN sing,masc'])
False
>>> _is_ambiguous(['NOUN sing,masc', 'NOUN masc,sing'])
True
>>> _is_ambiguous(['NOUN masc,sing', 'NOUN,masc sing'])
False
>>> _is_ambiguous(['NOUN masc,sing', 'NOUN,masc sing'], skip_space_ambiguity=False)
True
```

modules.name: pymorphy3.units.by_analogy
documentation:
Analogy analyzer units
----------------------

This module provides analyzer units that analyzes unknown words by looking
at how similar known words are analyzed.

classes.name: pymorphy3.units.by_analogy.KnownPrefixAnalyzer
documentation:
Parse the word by checking if it starts with a known prefix
and parsing the remainder.

Example: псевдокошка -> (псевдо) + кошка.

classes.name: pymorphy3.units.by_analogy.UnknownPrefixAnalyzer
documentation:
Parse the word by parsing only the word suffix
(with restrictions on prefix & suffix lengths).

Example: байткод -> (байт) + код

classes.name: pymorphy3.units.by_analogy.KnownSuffixAnalyzer

inner_classes.name: pymorphy3.units.by_analogy.KnownSuffixAnalyzer.FakeDictionary
documentation: This is just a DictionaryAnalyzer with different __repr__

Parse the word by checking how the words with similar suffixes
are parsed.

Example: бутявкать -> ...вкать

Dictionary analyzer unit
------------------------

Analyzer unit that analyzes word using dictionary.

classes.name: pymorphy3.units.base.BaseAnalyzerUnit

methods.name: pymorphy3.units.base.BaseAnalyzerUnit._get_param_names
documentation:
Get parameter names for the analyzer unit.
It works by introspecting `__init__` arguments.
`__init__` method must not use *args.

methods.name: pymorphy3.units.base.BaseAnalyzerUnit._get_params
documentation: Return a dict with the parameters for this analyzer unit.

Base class for analyzer units.

For parsing to work subclasses must implement `parse` method;
as an optimization they may also override `tag` method.

For inflection to work (this includes normalization) a subclass
must implement `normalized` and `get_lexeme` methods.

In __init__ method all parameters must be saved as instance variables
for analyzer unit to work.

methods.name: pymorphy3.units.base.AnalogyAnalyzerUnit.normalizer
documentation: A coroutine for normalization

methods.name: pymorphy3.units.base.AnalogyAnalyzerUnit.lexemizer
documentation: A coroutine for preparing lexemes

functions.name: pymorphy3.analyzer._iter_entry_points
documentation:
Finds entry points of installed packages, including ones installed
after the current process is started.

The main use case is to make `!pip install pymorphy3` work
within a Jupyter or Google Colab notebook.
See <https://github.com/kmike/pymorphy2/issues/131>

classes.name: pymorphy3.analyzer.MorphAnalyzer

methods.name: pymorphy3.analyzer.MorphAnalyzer.parse
documentation:
Analyze the word and return a list of {class}`pymorphy3.analyzer.Parse`
namedtuples:

> Parse(word, tag, normal_form, para_id, idx, \_score)

(or plain tuples if `result_type=None` was used in constructor).

methods.name: pymorphy3.analyzer.MorphAnalyzer.normal_forms
documentation: Return a list of word normal forms.

methods.name: pymorphy3.analyzer.MorphAnalyzer.get_lexeme
documentation: Return the lexeme this parse belongs to.
==== inflection ========

methods.name: pymorphy3.analyzer.MorphAnalyzer.iter_known_word_parses
documentation:
Return an iterator over parses of dictionary words that starts
with a given prefix (default empty prefix means "all words").

methods.name: pymorphy3.analyzer.MorphAnalyzer.word_is_known
documentation:
Check if a `word` is in the dictionary.

By default, some fuzziness is allowed, depending on a
dictionary - e.g. for Russian ё letters replaced with е are handled.
Pass `strict=True` to make matching strict (e.g. if it is
guaranteed the `word` has correct е/ё or г/ґ letters).

:::{note}
Dictionary words are not always correct words;
the dictionary also contains incorrect forms which
are commonly used. So for spellchecking tasks this
method should be used with extra care.
:::

methods.name: pymorphy3.analyzer.MorphAnalyzer.cyr2lat
documentation: Return Latin representation for `tag_or_grammeme` string

methods.name: pymorphy3.analyzer.MorphAnalyzer.lat2cyr
documentation: Return Cyrillic representation for `tag_or_grammeme` string

Morphological analyzer for Russian language.

For a given word it can find all possible inflectional paradigms
and thus compute all possible tags and normal forms.

Analyzer uses morphological word features and a lexicon
(dictionary compiled from XML available at OpenCorpora.org);
for unknown words heuristic algorithm is used.

Create a {class}`MorphAnalyzer` object:

```
>>> import pymorphy3
>>> morph = pymorphy3.MorphAnalyzer()
```

MorphAnalyzer uses dictionaries from `pymorphy2-dicts` package
(which can be installed via `pip install pymorphy2-dicts`).

Alternatively (e.g. if you have your own precompiled dictionaries),
either create `PYMORPHY2_DICT_PATH` environment variable
with a path to dictionaries, or pass `path` argument
to {class}`pymorphy3.MorphAnalyzer` constructor:

```
>>> morph = pymorphy3.MorphAnalyzer(path='/path/to/dictionaries') # doctest: +SKIP
```

By default, methods of this class return parsing results
as namedtuples {class}`Parse`. This has performance implications
under CPython, so if you need maximum speed then pass
`result_type=None` to make analyzer return plain unwrapped tuples:

```
>>> morph = pymorphy3.MorphAnalyzer(result_type=None)
```

Analyzer units that analyzes non-word tokes

methods.name: pymorphy3.units.by_shape._ShapeAnalyzer.check_shape
documentation: implement these 2 methods in a subclass:

classes.name: pymorphy3.units.by_shape.PunctuationAnalyzer

class_variables.name: pymorphy3.units.by_shape.PunctuationAnalyzer.TAG_STR_CYR
documentation: aot.ru uses this name

This analyzer tags punctuation marks as "PNCT".
Example: "," -> PNCT

classes.name: pymorphy3.units.by_shape.LatinAnalyzer
documentation:
This analyzer marks latin words with "LATN" tag.
Example: "pdf" -> LATN

classes.name: pymorphy3.units.by_shape.NumberAnalyzer
documentation:
This analyzer marks integer numbers with "NUMB,int" or "NUMB,real" tags.
Example: "12" -> NUMB,int; "12.4" -> NUMB,real

:::{note}
Don't confuse it with "NUMR": "тридцать" -> NUMR
:::

modules.name: pymorphy3.lang.uk._prefixes
documentation: Prefixes which don't change the word parse.
The list is from
https://github.com/languagetool-org/languagetool/blob/master/languagetool-language-modules/uk/src/main/resources/org/languagetool/resource/uk/dash_prefixes.txt

modules.name: pymorphy3.units.by_hyphen
documentation:
Analyzer units for unknown words with hyphens
---------------------------------------------

classes.name: pymorphy3.units.by_hyphen.HyphenSeparatedParticleAnalyzer
documentation:
Parse the word by analyzing it without
a particle after a hyphen.

Example: смотри-ка -> смотри + "-ка".

:::{note}
This analyzer doesn't remove particles from the result
so for normalization you may need to handle
particles at tokenization level.
:::

classes.name: pymorphy3.units.by_hyphen.HyphenAdverbAnalyzer
documentation:
Detect adverbs that starts with "по-".

Example: по-западному

classes.name: pymorphy3.units.by_hyphen.HyphenatedWordsAnalyzer

methods.name: pymorphy3.units.by_hyphen.HyphenatedWordsAnalyzer._parse_as_fixed_left
documentation:
Step 1: Assume that the left part is an immutable prefix.
Examples: интернет-магазин, воздушно-капельный

methods.name: pymorphy3.units.by_hyphen.HyphenatedWordsAnalyzer._parse_as_variable_both
documentation:
Step 2: if left and right can be parsed the same way,
then it may be the case that both parts should be inflected.
Examples: человек-гора, команд-участниц, компания-производитель

class_variables.name: pymorphy3.units.by_hyphen.HyphenatedWordsAnalyzer._CONSIDER_THE_SAME
documentation: TODO: add more grammemes

Parse the word by parsing its hyphen-separated parts.

Examples:

    * интернет-магазин -> "интернет-" + магазин
    * человек-гора -> человек + гора

documentation: Constants and configuration for Russian language.

functions.name: pymorphy3.cli.main
documentation: pymorphy3 is a morphological analyzer / inflection engine for Russian language.

functions.name: pymorphy3.cli._open_for_read
documentation: Open a file for reading

functions.name: pymorphy3.cli.show_dict_mem_usage
documentation: Show dictionary memory usage.

functions.name: pymorphy3.cli.parse
documentation:
Parse text from in_file; write output to out_file.
Both `in_file` and `out_file` must support unicode.

- If `tokenize` is False assume text is already tokenized - a token per

new line.
\* If `score` is True, include score in the output.
\* If `normal_form` is True, include normal form in the output.
\* If `tag` is True, include tags in the output.
\* If `newline` is True, write each result on a new line.
\* `cache_size` is a maximum number of entries in internal cache.
\* `thresh` is a minimum allowed parse score

functions.name: pymorphy3.cli._iter_tokens_tokenize
documentation: Return an iterator of input tokens; each line is tokenized

functions.name: pymorphy3.cli._iter_tokens_notokenize
documentation: Return an iterator of input tokens; each line is a single token

classes.name: pymorphy3.cli._TokenParserFormatter
documentation:
This class defines its `parse` method based on arguments passed.
Some ugly code is to make all ifs work only once, not for each token.
